item.title: PH launches fully digital apostille for PSA, CHED documents
item.description: The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) on Monday formally launched the Fully Digital Apostille System for PSA eCertificates and CHED electronic Certification, Authentication and Verification documents, eliminating paper apostilles for both document types effective immediately.

item.title: House OKs on final reading bill for emergency powers on fuel excise tax
item.description: The House of Representatives approved on third and final reading House Bill 8418 to authorize the president to suspend or reduce excise taxes on petroleum products during national or global economic emergencies.

item.title: PAOCC, CICC join forces vs illegal online lending, cyber scams
item.description: The Presidential Anti-Organized Crime Commission (PAOCC) and the Cybercrime Investigation and Coordinating Center (CICC) have strengthened their partnership to go after illegal online lending institutions and other cybercrime operations.

item.title: ‘Ligaw na bala’ mula sa firing range, tumawid sa bangko; sekyu, sugatan
item.description: Patuloy na tinutugis ng pulisya ang isang negosyante na nakabaril sa security guard ng bangko sa Barangay Pinagkaisahan, Quezon City noong Martes, Marso 10.

item.title: PH has 'indivisible sovereignty over Bajo de Masinloc,' alleged letter of concession posted by China Embassy 'without value' — DFA
item.description: The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) on Monday delivered a pointed rebuttal to the Chinese Embassy in Manila after it posted what it claimed was a 1990 letter by the late Philippine Ambassador to Germany Bienvenido A. Tan Jr. — a document Beijing used to prove Scarborough Shoal is outside PH.

item.title: Rhian Ramos to pursue possible legal action over driver’s assault claims
item.description: Actress Rhian Ramos said she is already looking into possible legal actions against her former driver, who alleged that she, Michelle Dee, and Sam Panlilio physically assaulted and illegally detained him back in January.

item.title: ‘I am deeply sorry’: Suntay’s wife breaks silence on husband's Anne Curtis remark
item.description: The wife of Rep. Bong Suntay has issued a public statement distancing herself and her children from the controversial remarks the Quezon City lawmaker made during a House impeachment hearing, after his comments referencing actress Anne Curtis sparked widespread backlash online.

item.title: No double standard in impeachment proceedings — House justice panel members
item.description: Leaders of the House Committee on Justice asserted there was no double standard in its deliberations on the impeachment complaints against the country’s two highest officials.

item.title: DSWD urges stronger cooperation among ASEAN states amid global tensions
item.description: Social Welfare Secretary Rex Gatchalian called on other members of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) for stronger cooperation amid global headwinds.

item.title: Man wanted for inciting to sedition surrenders to police
item.description: The Quezon City Police District on Thursday said it has successfully served a warrant of arrest against an individual wanted for inciting to sedition.
